_comment: Example BlockPatch — the only way an AI is allowed to modify a document. This shape matches BlockPatchSchema in TYPES.md §6. Use as a fixture for testing the LLM interface (M3).

content.text: EU tax credits expire Q4 2027. A go-decision by Q1 2027 is required to secure first-mover pricing.

_replace_example.reason: Tightened from 2 sentences to 1, as requested in comment c-001.

_remove_example.reason: Section overlap with executive summary; consolidating per comment c-002.

content.text: EU ETS phase-4 cap reductions accelerate from 2.2% to 4.3% annually starting 2027.

_insert_after_example.reason: Added regulatory context as suggested in comment c-003.

_usage_notes: A real BlockPatch object is one of the three shapes above (not the wrapper object shown here).
The `op` field is the discriminator — it determines which other fields are required.
For `replace` and `insert-after`, the `block` field must itself be a valid Block (matching the closed library).
The `reason` field is optional but recommended — it helps the consultant understand the AI's intent during review.
